chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.S-132 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections at flag "A". 2. For hearing of main case. ------ 30.05.2025 Mr. Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Munwar Ali Abbasi, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh a/w Inspector Abdul Ghafoor Chutto, S.H.O. P.S. Taluka and SIP Ali Dost Chandio on behalf of S.S.P. Larkana. ------ Through this petition, the petitioner claims following relief(s): a. That this Honourable Court may be pleased to issue writ directing the respondents No. 1 and 2 not to cause harassment to the petitioner party at the behest of private respondents and to strictly act in accordance with law. b. That this Honourable Court further be pleased to issue writ directing the respondent No.3 to provide all the legal and constitutional protection to the life, liberty property of the petitioner and his family member and save them from ill intentions and unjustified harassment, which is being caused by the respondents No.1 and 2 and private respondents and further respondents No.1 & 2 may be directed not to register any false case against the petitioner on the instance of private respondents No.4 & 5. Mr. Dildar Ali Chandio, advocate files vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.4 & 5, taken on record. He undertakes that the respondents will not cause any harassment against the petitioner. The statements filed on behalf of the official respondents are also taken on record. Inspector Abdul Ghafoor Chutto, S.H.O. P.S. Taluka, present, submits that they will act strictly in accordance with law and protection will be provided to the petitioner as and when required.

P.S. Taluka, present, submits that they will act strictly in accordance with law and protection will be provided to the petitioner as and when required. Learned counsel for the petitioner being satisfied with the statements seeks disposal of instant petition. Learned Assistant Advocate General, Sindh has no objection. Order accordingly. Judge Manzoor
